newsBriefing: Major shifts in the sports landscape dominated the headlines today, ranging from historic coaching milestones and high-stakes international drama to significant roster shakeups just days before the NCAA tournament. Steve Kerr etched his name deeper into NBA history by becoming the fourth-fastest coach to reach 600 wins as his Warriors snapped a losing streak, while the league at large moved closer to a new era with a pending vote on expansion into Seattle and Las Vegas. The college basketball world was rocked by the arrest of Alabama star Aden Holloway on felony drug charges, leaving the Crimson Tide to scramble for a new identity without their second-leading scorer right as March Madness begins. Meanwhile, Michigan's top seed looks even more impressive following revelations that star Yaxel Lendeborg turned down a massive $7-9 million NIL offer from Kentucky to stay with the Wolverines. On the international stage, Venezuela celebrated a historic first by rallying past Italy to reach the World Baseball Classic final against the U.S., while a geopolitical rift has cast doubt on the upcoming World Cup, with Iran requesting to move its matches from American soil to Mexico due to security concerns. NFL news rounded out the day as the Kansas City Chiefs fortified their quarterback depth by acquiring Justin Fields from the Jets to back up an injured Patrick Mahomes, and the San Francisco 49ers continued their wide receiver overhaul by signing veteran Christian Kirk to a one-year deal.
